reference: FIDIC Procurement Guidelines, SAP MM Sourcing Strategy

description: Any special procurement requirements such as local content mandates, sustainability certifications (LEED), HSE requirements, or regulatory compliance needs.
reference: LEED (Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design), HSE (Health Safety and Environment)

description: Indicates whether suppliers must be prequalified before being invited to bid for this procurement.
Required warranty or defects liability period in months for the procured materials or equipment.
Unique identifier for the vendor quotation record. Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to bid.boq. Business justification: Vendor quotations are solicited against BOQ items in construction procurement. Linking quotation to BOQ enables price benchmarking against tendered quantities — a core bid evaluation and cost control
Foreign key linking to procurement.material_catalog. Business justification: Link vendor quotation material to master catalog for normalization and remove redundant material_id column.
Reference to the RFQ document that this quotation responds to. Links the vendor response back to the procurement request.

Foreign key linking to material.specification. Business justification: Vendor quotation technical compliance evaluation in construction requires direct comparison against the material specification (tensile strength, compressive strength, standard code). This FK enables
Reference to the vendor master record submitting this quotation. Identifies the supplier providing the quote.
Number of supporting documents attached to the quotation (technical datasheets, certifications, test reports, etc.).
Procurement team recommendation on whether to award the PO to this vendor based on evaluation results.
Vendor-declared exceptions or deviations from RFQ commercial terms (payment, delivery, penalties, etc.). Must be evaluated during bid tabulation.
Three-letter ISO country code indicating where the material is manufactured or sourced. Relevant for customs, duties, and local content requirements.
System timestamp when the quotation record was first created in the procurement system. Audit trail for record creation.
Three-letter ISO currency code for the quoted prices (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, AED). Critical for multi-currency bid comparison.
Number of calendar days from PO issuance to material delivery at site. Critical for project scheduling and CPM integration.
Incoterms delivery terms defining responsibility transfer point (e.g., EXW, FOB, CIF, DDP). Impacts freight cost allocation and risk.
Vendor-declared exceptions, deviations, or clarifications to the RFQ technical specifications. Critical for commercial evaluation and risk assessment.
Percentage discount offered by vendor from list price or previous quotation. Used for price negotiation and historical benchmarking.
Date when the quotation evaluation was completed and recommendation finalized.
Free-text notes and comments from the evaluation team regarding technical compliance, commercial terms, or vendor performance considerations.
Composite evaluation score assigned during bid tabulation based on price, technical compliance, delivery, and vendor performance. Used for award recommendation.
Quoted freight or transportation cost if not included in unit price. Depends on delivery terms (Incoterms).
System timestamp when the quotation record was last updated. Tracks changes during evaluation and negotiation cycles.
Vendor-provided description of the material or service being quoted. May include brand, model, or specification details.
Vendor-offered payment terms (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 50% advance + 50% on delivery, LC at sight). Impacts cash flow and working capital.
Vendor-assigned unique reference number for this quotation. External business identifier used for correspondence and tracking.
Current lifecycle status of the vendor quotation in the evaluation and award process.
Quantity of material or service the vendor is quoting for. Must align with RFQ requested quantity.
Date and time when the vendor submitted the quotation response. Critical for evaluating timeliness and compliance with RFQ deadlines.
Applicable tax amount (VAT, GST, sales tax) on the quoted price. May vary by jurisdiction and project location.
Assessment of whether the quoted material meets the technical specifications defined in the RFQ. Evaluated by engineering team.
Total quoted price for the full quantity (unit price × quantity). May include or exclude taxes and freight depending on quotation terms.
Unit of measure for the quoted quantity (e.g., EA, M, KG, TON, M3, HR). Must match material master UOM or include conversion factor.
Vendor-quoted price per unit of measure. Base price before taxes, freight, or other charges.
Date until which the vendor commits to honor the quoted price and terms. Critical for award decision timing.
Date from which the quoted price and terms become valid. Typically the quotation submission date.
Duration of warranty coverage offered by the vendor in months. Aligns with DLP (Defects Liability Period) requirements.
Detailed warranty terms and conditions including coverage scope, exclusions, and claim procedures.
Unique identifier for the vendor record. Primary key for the vendor master data product. System-generated surrogate key used across all procurement and supply chain transactions.
SAP vendor account number assigned in SAP MM Vendor Master. External business identifier used in purchase orders, invoices, and payment processing. Unique across the ERP system.
First line of the vendor's registered business address. Typically contains street number and street name. Used for correspondence, legal notices, and payment remittance.
Second line of the vendor's business address. Contains suite, floor, building, or other secondary address information. Nullable if not applicable.

Vendor's reported annual revenue in the vendor's default currency. Used for vendor size classification, capacity assessment, and financial stability evaluation. Typically self-reported or obtained from financial statements.

Date when the vendor was approved for procurement transactions following successful completion of prequalification, due diligence, and compliance checks. Nullable for prospective vendors not yet approved.

Outcome of the most recent vendor audit. Passed indicates full compliance; passed with conditions indicates minor findings requiring corrective action; failed indicates major non-conformances; not audited indicates no audit has been conducted.
Vendor's bank account number for electronic payment receipt. Highly sensitive financial information used for ACH and wire transfer processing. Encrypted at rest and in transit.
Name of the financial institution where the vendor maintains their primary business account for payment receipt. Used for electronic payment processing and wire transfers.
Bank routing number, SWIFT code, or IBAN for the vendor's bank account. Used to route electronic payments to the correct financial institution. Format varies by country and payment network.

Maximum bonding capacity available to the vendor in the vendor's default currency. Represents the total value of performance bonds and payment bonds the vendor can secure from their surety. Critical for subcontractor prequalification and large contract awards.
City or municipality where the vendor's registered business address is located. Used for geographic analysis, local sourcing initiatives, and logistics planning.

Primary business classification of the vendor based on the type of goods or services provided. Determines procurement workflows, approval hierarchies, and contract templates. Material suppliers provide construction materials; equipment rental provides machinery; specialist subcontractors perform MEP, civil, or structural work; general contractors manage construction execution; professional services include design, engineering, and consulting; utility providers supply power, water, and telecommunications.
Three-letter ISO country code for the vendor's registered business location. Used for international trade compliance, currency determination, and cross-border procurement regulations.
Timestamp when this vendor record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and vendor relationship tenure analysis. System-generated and immutable.

Credit rating assigned by credit rating agencies (Dun & Bradstreet, Moody's, S&P) reflecting the vendor's financial stability and creditworthiness. Used for vendor risk assessment and payment term determination. NR indicates not rated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: aaa|aa|a|bbb|bb|b|ccc|cc|c|d|nr — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Three-letter ISO currency code for the vendor's default transaction currency. Determines the currency used for purchase orders, invoices, and payments. Used for foreign exchange exposure analysis.

Diversity certification status for the vendor. MBE (Minority Business Enterprise), WBE (Women Business Enterprise), DBE (Disadvantaged Business Enterprise), SDVOSB (Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business), HUBZone (Historically Underutilized Business Zone). Used for diversity spend reporting and compliance with client diversity requirements.

Nine-digit DUNS number issued by Dun & Bradstreet for unique identification of business entities. Used for credit checks, vendor risk assessment, and compliance with government contracting requirements.
Total number of employees working for the vendor organization. Used for vendor capacity assessment, small business classification, and resource availability evaluation.

Geographic regions or markets where the vendor operates and can provide services. Free-text field listing countries, states, or regions. Used for project-specific vendor selection and logistics planning.

Certificate number for the vendor's current general liability and professional indemnity insurance policy. Used to verify insurance coverage before contract execution and during vendor compliance audits.
Expiration date of the vendor's current insurance certificate. Monitored to ensure continuous insurance coverage throughout the contract period. Triggers renewal reminders and compliance alerts.

Date of the most recent vendor audit or compliance review. Audits assess quality systems, safety practices, financial controls, and contract compliance. Used to schedule periodic re-audits and maintain vendor qualification status.

Timestamp when this vendor record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the vendor master record. Used for change tracking, data quality monitoring, and synchronization with downstream systems.

Preferred payment method for remitting payment to the vendor. ACH for automated clearing house electronic transfer; wire transfer for same-day international payments; check for traditional paper payments; credit card for small purchases; letter of credit for international trade.

Standard payment terms code defining the payment schedule and discount structure for this vendor. Examples include NET30 (net 30 days), NET60, 2/10NET30 (2% discount if paid within 10 days, otherwise net 30). Drives accounts payable processing and cash flow planning.
Postal or ZIP code for the vendor's business address. Used for mail delivery, logistics planning, and geographic segmentation. Format varies by country.

Boolean flag indicating whether this vendor has preferred vendor status. Preferred vendors receive priority consideration for bid invitations, expedited approval processes, and favorable payment terms based on proven performance and strategic relationship.

Composite score from the vendor prequalification process evaluating financial stability, technical capability, safety record, quality performance, and past project experience. Scale typically 0-100. Used for vendor ranking and bid invitation decisions.
Email address of the primary vendor contact. Used for purchase order transmission, RFQ distribution, invoice queries, and general procurement communication.
Full name of the primary business contact at the vendor organization. This individual serves as the main point of contact for procurement, contract administration, and operational coordination.
Primary telephone number for the vendor contact. Used for urgent procurement matters, delivery coordination, and issue resolution. Includes country code and extension where applicable.

Quality management system certifications held by the vendor. Common certifications include ISO 9001 (Quality Management), ISO 14001 (Environmental Management), ISO 45001 (Occupational Health and Safety). Multiple certifications separated by semicolons.
Date when the vendor was first registered in the vendor master system. Marks the beginning of the vendor relationship and is used for vendor tenure analysis and anniversary tracking.
State, province, or region where the vendor is located. Used for tax jurisdiction determination, regional sourcing analysis, and compliance with local content requirements.

Planned or actual date when vendor suspension will be or was lifted. Nullable for indefinite suspensions or vendors that have never been suspended. Used for reinstatement planning and compliance tracking.

Reason for vendor suspension or blocking if vendor_status is suspended or blocked. Examples include quality issues, safety violations, contract breaches, financial instability, or ethical violations. Nullable for active vendors.
Date when vendor suspension or blocking became effective. Used to track suspension duration and trigger review processes. Nullable for vendors that have never been suspended.

Government-issued tax identification number for the vendor. Used for tax reporting, 1099 processing, and compliance with tax regulations. Format varies by jurisdiction (EIN in USA, VAT number in EU, GST number in other regions).
Full legal name of the vendor organization as registered with tax authorities. Used on contracts, purchase orders, and payment documents. Must match legal registration documents.

Current lifecycle status of the vendor in the vendor master. Approved vendors can receive purchase orders; prospective vendors are under evaluation; suspended vendors have temporary restrictions; blocked vendors cannot receive new orders; inactive vendors are no longer used; under_review vendors are being re-qualified.
Primary key for vendor_evaluation
Identifier of the project for which this vendor evaluation was conducted. Applicable for project-specific evaluations tied to bid eligibility.

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Vendor performance evaluations in construction measure compliance with specific regulatory obligations (HSE regulations, environmental standards). Linking vendor_evaluation to regulatory_obligation en
Identifier of the vendor being evaluated. Links to the vendor master record in the procurement system.

Foreign key linking to site.work_front. Business justification: Work-front-level vendor performance evaluation: vendor evaluations in construction are conducted for specific work fronts where the vendor performed (e.g., earthworks front, concrete front). This link
Date when the vendor was added to the AVL (Approved Vendor List) following successful pre-qualification. Null for vendors not yet approved.

Boolean indicator (True/False) determining whether the vendor is currently eligible to receive bid invitations for RFP (Request for Proposal) and RFQ (Request for Quotation) based on evaluation outcome and qualification status.

Maximum contract value (in USD) for which the vendor can provide performance and payment bonds. Critical for pre-qualification and determines eligibility for large-scale EPC (Engineering Procurement and Construction) projects.
Numerical score (0-100) evaluating the vendor's adherence to contract terms, invoicing accuracy, payment terms compliance, and commercial documentation completeness.
Detailed description of corrective actions required from the vendor to address performance gaps, quality issues, HSE violations, or compliance deficiencies identified during evaluation.
Target date by which the vendor must complete and demonstrate closure of required corrective actions. Failure to meet this date may result in suspension or disqualification.
Boolean indicator (True/False) signaling that the vendor must implement corrective actions to address identified deficiencies before qualification can be maintained or upgraded.
Timestamp when the vendor evaluation record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle tracking.
Date when the evaluation was formally completed and scored. Represents the business event timestamp for the assessment.
Free-text field capturing additional observations, strengths, weaknesses, and contextual information from the evaluator that supplements the quantitative scores.
Business identifier for the vendor evaluation record. Format: VE-YYYYMMDD followed by sequence number.
End date of the performance evaluation period. For periodic reviews, defines the close of the assessment window.
Start date of the performance evaluation period. For periodic reviews, defines the beginning of the assessment window. For pre-qualification, represents the date evaluation commenced.
Current lifecycle status of the vendor evaluation. Tracks progression from draft through approval or rejection.

Type of vendor evaluation being conducted. Pre-qualification for new vendors entering AVL (Approved Vendor List), periodic review for ongoing performance assessment, project-specific for bid eligibility, re-qualification for expired certifications, or incident-triggered for corrective action follow-up.

Numerical score (0-100) assessing the vendor's financial stability based on credit rating, liquidity ratios, debt-to-equity, and financial statement analysis. Critical for pre-qualification and bonding capacity assessment.

business_glossary_term: Health Safety and Environment (HSE) Incident Count
description: Total number of HSE (Health Safety and Environment) incidents (including LTI, near-misses, and safety violations) involving the vendor during the evaluation period.

business_glossary_term: Health Safety and Environment (HSE) Rating Score
description: Numerical score (0-100) assessing the vendor's HSE (Health Safety and Environment) performance including TRIR (Total Recordable Incident Rate), LTI (Lost Time Injury) frequency, safety certifications (ISO 45001), and HSE management system maturity.
Boolean indicator (True/False) confirming that the vendor's general liability, professional indemnity, and workers' compensation insurance policies have been verified and meet project requirements.
Timestamp when the vendor evaluation record was last updated. Audit trail for change tracking and data freshness monitoring.
Total number of NCR (Non-Conformance Report) incidents raised against the vendor during the evaluation period. Lower counts indicate better quality performance.
Percentage (0-100) of deliveries completed on or before the scheduled delivery date during the evaluation period. Key KPI for schedule performance and reliability.

Composite numerical score (0-100) representing the weighted average of all evaluation dimensions (financial, technical, HSE, quality, delivery, responsiveness, commercial). Determines overall vendor performance grade.

Letter grade (A through F) assigned based on overall KPI rating. A=Excellent (90-100), B=Good (80-89), C=Satisfactory (70-79), D=Marginal (60-69), F=Unsatisfactory (<60). Drives vendor tier classification and bid invitation priority.
Date when the vendor's qualification status expires and requires renewal through re-evaluation. Typically set 12-24 months from evaluation date for qualified vendors.
Resulting qualification status of the vendor based on evaluation outcome. Determines vendor eligibility for bid invitations and PO (Purchase Order) issuance.
Percentage (0-100) of delivered materials or services that passed quality inspection on first submission without NCR (Non-Conformance Report) during the evaluation period.

Numerical score (0-100) evaluating the vendor's quality management certifications (ISO 9001), QA/QC (Quality Assurance/Quality Control) processes, inspection capabilities, and adherence to ITP (Inspection and Test Plan) requirements.

Numerical score (0-100) assessing the vendor's responsiveness to RFI (Request for Information), RFQ (Request for Quotation), change orders, and issue resolution. Measures communication effectiveness and turnaround time.

Operational system from which the evaluation data was sourced. Procore for project-specific evaluations, SAP MM for vendor master and procurement data, Intelex for HSE performance data, or manual for offline assessments.
Numerical score (0-100) evaluating the vendor's technical competence, equipment availability, workforce skills, past project experience, and ability to meet technical specifications.
Unique identifier for the vendor qualification record. Primary key for the vendor qualification entity.

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Vendor qualifications in construction are mandated by specific regulatory obligations (HSE regulations, environmental standards). Linking vendor_qualification to the governing regulatory_obligation en

Foreign key linking to workforce.skill_trade. Business justification: Construction vendor qualification records specify which skill trades a subcontractor is approved to perform. This structured link drives subcontractor selection during workforce planning, bid invitati
Reference to the vendor master record being qualified. Links to the vendor entity in the procurement domain.
Date when the vendor qualification was formally approved and the vendor was added to the AVL (Approved Vendor List).

Comma-separated list of material categories the vendor is qualified to supply. Examples include concrete, steel, MEP (Mechanical Electrical and Plumbing) equipment, formwork, aggregates. Aligns with MTO (Material Take-Off) classification.

Comma-separated list of service types the vendor is qualified to provide. Examples include earthworks, structural steel erection, electrical installation, HVAC installation, concrete placement, surveying.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for bonding capacity limit. Typically USD for US-based projects.

Maximum bonding capacity available from the vendor's surety company. Determines the maximum contract value the vendor can undertake. Critical for GC (General Contractor) and major subcontractor qualifications.
Timestamp when the vendor qualification record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for compliance and governance.

Composite financial health assessment score ranging from 0.00 to 100.00. Based on credit rating, liquidity ratios, debt-to-equity, and financial statement analysis. Minimum threshold typically 60.00 for approval.
Geographic regions or countries where the vendor is qualified to operate. Comma-separated list of ISO 3166 country codes or region names. Important for multi-site and international projects.

business_glossary_term: Health Safety and Environment (HSE) Performance Rating
description: HSE (Health Safety and Environment) performance rating based on TRIR (Total Recordable Incident Rate), LTI (Lost Time Injury) frequency, safety audit results, and compliance with OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Administration) standards. Critical factor for vendor approval.
Expiry date of the vendor's insurance certificate. Procurement tracks this to ensure continuous coverage throughout contract execution.
General liability insurance coverage limit. Minimum requirements typically range from $1M to $10M depending on project size and risk profile.
Indicates whether valid workers compensation insurance has been verified. Mandatory for all vendors providing on-site labor per OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Administration) requirements.
Certificate number for ISO 14001 Environmental Management System certification.

Indicates whether the vendor holds valid ISO 14001 Environmental Management System certification. Increasingly required for LEED (Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design) projects and environmentally sensitive sites.
Expiry date of ISO 14001 certification. Critical for projects with EPA (Environmental Protection Agency) compliance requirements.
Certificate number for ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management System certification.

business_glossary_term: ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Certified
description: Indicates whether the vendor holds valid ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management System certification. Demonstrates commitment to worker safety and HSE (Health Safety and Environment) excellence.
Expiry date of ISO 45001 certification. Monitored to ensure ongoing HSE (Health Safety and Environment) compliance.
Certificate number for ISO 9001 Quality Management System certification. Used for verification with certification body.
Indicates whether the vendor holds valid ISO 9001 Quality Management System certification. Often mandatory for critical material suppliers and major subcontractors.
Expiry date of ISO 9001 certification. Procurement monitors this to ensure vendors maintain valid certification throughout contract execution.
Timestamp when the vendor qualification record was last updated. Tracks changes to qualification status, scores, certifications, or other attributes.
LTI (Lost Time Injury) frequency rate calculated as (number of LTIs × 1,000,000) / total hours worked. Measures severity of safety incidents. Target is typically below 1.0 for qualified vendors.
Free-text notes capturing additional qualification details, special conditions, limitations, or observations from the assessment process. Used by procurement team for decision support.
Percentage of past deliveries completed on or before scheduled date. Critical metric for CPM (Critical Path Method) schedule-sensitive projects. Target is typically above 90.00%.

Composite past performance score ranging from 0.00 to 100.00. Based on historical project delivery, quality, schedule adherence, cost control, and client satisfaction. Derived from completed project evaluations and NCR (Non-Conformance Report) history.
Date when the qualification assessment was conducted. Includes site visits, document reviews, financial analysis, and technical capability evaluation.
Primary category of goods or services the vendor is qualified to supply. MEP (Mechanical Electrical and Plumbing) is a specialized category for building systems contractors.
Date on which the vendor qualification expires and requires renewal. Typically set 1-3 years from start date depending on vendor category and risk profile.
Business identifier for the vendor qualification record. Externally visible unique reference number used in procurement communications and AVL (Approved Vendor List) documentation.
Effective date from which the vendor qualification becomes valid. Vendors can only receive PO (Purchase Order) assignments on or after this date.

Current lifecycle status of the vendor qualification. Approved vendors are eligible for PO (Purchase Order) issuance. Suspended vendors require re-qualification. Expired qualifications require renewal.

Type of qualification process being conducted. Initial for new vendors, renewal for periodic re-assessment, re-qualification for vendors returning after suspension, conditional for limited-scope approval, emergency for urgent project needs.

Historical quality defect rate expressed as percentage of deliverables requiring rework or NCR (Non-Conformance Report) issuance. Lower values indicate better quality performance. Target is typically below 5.00%.

Detailed explanation if the vendor qualification was rejected. Includes specific deficiencies in financial health, technical capability, HSE (Health Safety and Environment) performance, or documentation.
Date when the vendor qualification was suspended. Suspended vendors cannot receive new PO (Purchase Order) assignments until re-qualified.
Reason for suspending an approved vendor qualification. Common causes include safety incidents, quality failures, financial distress, contract breaches, or regulatory violations.

Assessment score of vendor technical capabilities ranging from 0.00 to 100.00. Evaluates equipment, workforce skills, past project complexity, and technical certifications. Minimum threshold typically 70.00 for complex EPC (Engineering Procurement and Construction) projects.

TRIR (Total Recordable Incident Rate) calculated as (number of recordable incidents × 200,000) / total hours worked. Industry benchmark for construction is typically below 3.0. Lower values indicate better safety performance.
Unique surrogate identifier for the project cost account (control account) record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Project Systems; Viewpoint Vista Job Costing

description: Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Cost accounts for subcontracted scope must be linked to the governing agreement for committed cost tracking, retention calculation, and contract financial reporting. The is_subcontract_scope flag on c
Reference to the parent construction project to which this cost account belongs. Links cost account to the project master record for project-level cost roll-up and reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Project Systems (PS Module); Oracle Primavera P6

description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Construction ERP systems (SAP PS) require cost accounts to map directly to GL accounts for actual cost posting and financial close. The cost_account.gl_account_code is a denormalized reference. A prop

Foreign key linking to project.phase. Business justification: cost_account currently stores phase_code as a STRING reference. Adding phase_id FK normalizes this to a proper relational link to the phase table. The phase_code string can be retrieved via JOIN to ph

Foreign key linking to project.project_baseline. Business justification: Cost accounts (control accounts) are established against an approved project baseline. The approved_budget_amount, original_budget_amount on cost_account correspond to a specific baseline revision. Ad

Foreign key linking to project.project_change_order. Business justification: cost_account has change_order_amount attribute tracking the cumulative CO impact on the control account. Adding project_change_order_id FK links the cost account to the specific change order that drov

Foreign key linking to site.site. Business justification: Cost accounts are tracked per site for multi-site projects. Site-level cost reporting (actual vs budget per site) is a standard project controls deliverable required for client reporting, SAP cost cen

Foreign key linking to bid.trade_package. Business justification: Cost accounts are structured to align with trade packages for subcontract cost control. Construction cost controllers track committed, actual, and forecast costs per trade package. is_subcontract_scop

Reference to the Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) element at which this cost account is positioned. The cost account represents the intersection of a WBS node and a cost code, enabling granular budget control per WBS deliverable.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PS WBS Element; Oracle Primavera P6 WBS; PMI PMBOK WBS Standard

description: Human-readable description of the cost account scope, such as 'Structural Concrete — Foundation Works' or 'MEP Subcontract — Electrical Rough-In'. Used in cost reports, BOQ reconciliation, and EVM reporting.
reference: Viewpoint Vista Job Costing; SAP S/4HANA PS; AACE International

description: Current lifecycle status of the cost account. Active accounts accept cost postings; on_hold accounts are temporarily frozen pending review; closed accounts are complete and locked; cancelled accounts are voided; pending_approval accounts await PMO authorization before activation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PS Account Status; PMO Governance Framework; IFRS 15

description: Total actual cost incurred and posted to this cost account to date, including paid invoices, posted labor timesheets, and equipment charges. Equivalent to Actual Cost of Work Performed (ACWP) in EVM. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI/CO and Viewpoint Vista job cost ledger.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-CO Actual Costs; Viewpoint Vista Job Costing; PMI PMBOK EVM; IFRS 15

description: Current approved budget for this cost account inclusive of all approved Change Orders (COs). Equivalent to Budget at Completion (BAC) in EVM terminology. Updated each time a CO is approved and posted to the cost account. The primary budget control figure used by project cost controllers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PS Approved Budget; AACE International; PMI PMBOK EVM; IFRS 15

description: Planned completion date for the scope of work associated with this cost account as established in the approved project baseline schedule. Used for schedule performance tracking and Earned Value Management (EVM) analysis.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Baseline Schedule; PMI PMBOK; SAP S/4HANA PS

description: Planned start date for the scope of work associated with this cost account as established in the approved project baseline schedule. Used for Planned Value (PV) calculation and schedule variance analysis in EVM reporting.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Baseline Schedule; PMI PMBOK; SAP S/4HANA PS

description: Budgeted cost per unit of measure for this cost account (Approved Budget divided by Budgeted Quantity). Used for unit rate benchmarking, bid analysis, and productivity monitoring. Expressed in the cost account currency per unit of measure.
reference: AACE International; Viewpoint Vista Job Costing; HCSS HeavyJob

description: Cumulative value of all approved Change Orders (COs) posted to this cost account. Represents the delta between the original budget and the current approved budget. Tracked separately to support contract administration and CO impact analysis.
reference: Procore Change Orders; SAP S/4HANA PS; FIDIC Contract Conditions; Contract Administration

description: Total value of contractual commitments against this cost account, including issued Purchase Orders (POs) and subcontract awards not yet invoiced. Represents financial obligations that will become actual costs. Critical for cash flow forecasting and cost-to-complete analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchase Orders; Viewpoint Vista Committed Cost; AACE International

description: Contingency budget allocated to this cost account to cover identified risks and uncertainties within the defined scope. Managed separately from the base budget and released by the project manager upon risk materialization. Supports risk-adjusted cost forecasting.
reference: AACE International Risk Analysis; PMI PMBOK Risk Management; SAP S/4HANA PS

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the cost account within the project. Derived from the job cost coding structure in Viewpoint Vista and SAP PS. Used on timesheets, purchase orders, and subcontract invoices for cost allocation.
reference: Viewpoint Vista Job Costing; SAP S/4HANA Cost Element; AACE International Cost Engineering

description: SAP Cost Center code associated with this cost account, representing the organizational unit responsible for the costs. Used for overhead allocation, departmental P&L reporting, and internal charge-back processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Center Accounting; IFRS/GAAP

description: Standardized cost code from the company's master cost code library, representing the work activity or resource category (e.g., '03300' for cast-in-place concrete, '16000' for electrical). Used for cross-project benchmarking and historical cost analysis. Aligns with CSI MasterFormat or company-specific coding structure.
reference: CSI MasterFormat; HCSS HeavyJob Cost Coding; Viewpoint Vista Cost Code

description: Estimated remaining cost required to complete the scope of work for this cost account. Calculated as Forecast Cost at Completion minus Actual Cost to Date. Used by cost controllers to project future cash requirements and update project financial forecasts.
reference: PMI PMBOK EVM; AACE International; SAP S/4HANA PS

description: Primary classification of the cost account by resource type: labor (direct workforce), material (permanent and consumable materials), equipment (plant and machinery), subcontract (third-party subcontractor scope), or overhead (indirect project costs). Drives cost segregation in EVM and P&L reporting.
reference: AACE International Recommended Practice 10S-90; SAP S/4HANA Cost Element Category; Viewpoint Vista Cost Type

description: Variance between the approved budget and the forecast cost at completion for this cost account (Approved Budget minus Forecast Cost at Completion). A negative value indicates a cost overrun; positive indicates an underspend. Key metric for project cost control reporting and PMO governance.
reference: PMI PMBOK EVM Cost Variance; AACE International; SAP S/4HANA PS Variance

description: Earned Value Management (EVM) Cost Performance Index for this cost account, calculated as Earned Value divided by Actual Cost (EV/AC). A CPI below 1.0 indicates cost overrun; above 1.0 indicates cost efficiency. Stored at the cost account level to support drill-down analysis from project-level CPI reporting.
reference: PMI PMBOK EVM; AACE International; SAP S/4HANA PS EVM

description: Timestamp when this cost account record was first created in the source system or ingested into the Silver Layer. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail; Data Governance Framework; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this cost account (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, AUD). Supports multi-currency project reporting for international EPC and JV projects.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards

description: Budgeted cost of work performed (BCWP) for this cost account, representing the value of completed work expressed in budget terms. Core EVM metric used to calculate Cost Performance Index (CPI) and Schedule Performance Index (SPI) at the cost account level.
reference: PMI PMBOK EVM; AACE International; SAP S/4HANA PS EVM

description: Project cost controller's current best estimate of the total cost to complete this cost account, including actual costs to date plus estimated cost to complete (ETC). Equivalent to Estimate at Completion (EAC) in EVM. Updated periodically during project cost reviews.
reference: AACE International Recommended Practice; PMI PMBOK EVM EAC; SAP S/4HANA PS Forecast

description: Current forecast completion date for the scope of work associated with this cost account. Updated during project schedule reviews. Used to assess Extension of Time (EOT) impacts and project completion forecasting.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6; SAP S/4HANA PS; FIDIC EOT Provisions

description: Current forecast start date for the scope of work associated with this cost account, updated during project schedule reviews. Compared against the baseline start date to identify schedule slippage and its cost impact.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6; SAP S/4HANA PS; PMI PMBOK

description: Indicates whether this cost account is priced on a lump sum basis (True) rather than a unit rate or reimbursable basis (False). Lump sum accounts are managed differently in terms of progress measurement and payment certification.

description: Indicates whether the scope of work for this cost account is executed by a subcontractor (True) or by the General Contractor's (GC) own forces (False). Drives subcontract management workflows, retention tracking, and payment certification processes.

description: The baseline budget amount for this cost account as established at project sanction or Notice to Proceed (NTP). description: Physical percent complete of the scope of work associated with this cost account, as assessed by the project team or quantity surveyor. Expressed as a value between 0.00 and 100.00. Used to calculate Earned Value and assess progress against the baseline schedule.

description: Original budgeted quantity of work for this cost account as derived from the Bill of Quantities (BOQ) or Material Take-Off (MTO). description: The data date or cut-off date as of which the cost account financial figures (actual cost, earned value, planned value, forecast) are reported. Aligns with the project monthly cost report cycle and EVM data date in Oracle Primavera P6.

description: Contractual retention percentage applied to payments against this cost account, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.00 for 5%). Retention is withheld from subcontractor and supplier payments until practical completion or Defects Liability Period (DLP) expiry.

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this cost account record was sourced: SAP S/4HANA Project Systems, Viewpoint Vista Job Costing, HCSS HeavyJob, Oracle Primavera P6, or manually entered. description: Earned Value Management (EVM) Schedule Performance Index for this cost account, calculated as Earned Value divided by Planned Value (EV/PV). description: Unit of measure for the budgeted and actual quantities on this cost account (e.g., HR for hours, M3 for cubic metres, TON for tonnes, LM for linear metres, EA for each, LS for lump sum). Aligns with the BOQ and MTO unit conventions.

Job title or designation of the contractor representative who signed the handover certificate (e.g., Project Director, Site Manager, Construction Manager). Validates the authority level of the signatory.

business_glossary_term: Defects Liability Period (DLP) Duration in Days
description: The contractually agreed duration of the Defects Liability Period (DLP) expressed in calendar days. Typically 365 days (12 months) for standard construction contracts but may vary by contract type and jurisdiction.

The date on which the Defects Liability Period (DLP) expires, after which the contractor's obligation to remedy defects under the contract ceases. Calculated from DLP start date plus the contractually agreed DLP duration.

The date on which the Defects Liability Period (DLP) commences, typically aligned with the client acceptance date. Marks the beginning of the contractor's obligation to remedy defects at no additional cost.

Full name of the Engineer (or Engineer's Representative) under FIDIC contracts who countersigns the handover certificate. Applicable under FIDIC Red/Yellow/Silver Book delivery models where an independent Engineer administers the contract.

Total number of Extension of Time (EOT) days granted and applied to the contractual completion date for the scope covered by this handover certificate. Affects LD liability calculation and schedule performance reporting.

Indicates whether Factory Acceptance Testing (FAT) has been completed for all equipment and systems included in the handover scope. FAT is a prerequisite for handover in EPC and MEP-intensive projects.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 8.6; IEC Factory Acceptance Standards

description: Classification of the handover certificate indicating the nature and extent of the transfer of works. Partial: a defined portion of works; Sectional: a contractually defined section; Substantial Completion: works are practically complete with minor outstanding items; Final: all works including punch list resolved; Provisional: conditional acceptance pending outstanding items.
reference: FIDIC Conditions of Contract Clause 10; IBC Completion Standards

business_glossary_term: Health Safety and Environment (HSE) Clearance Obtained
description: Indicates whether all required Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) clearances and regulatory approvals have been obtained prior to handover. Includes OSHA compliance, environmental permits, and occupancy certificates.
reference: OSHA Standards; ISO 45001; ISO 14001; EPA Regulations

description: The date on which the handover certificate was formally issued by the contractor to the client. This is the principal business event date (BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category) representing the real-world transfer initiation event.

Indicates whether all Inspection and Test Plan (ITP) hold points and witness points for the works scope have been completed and signed off prior to handover. ITP completion is a mandatory QA/QC prerequisite for handover.

The date on which the contractor's exposure to Liquidated Damages (LD) for delay ceases, typically aligned with the handover acceptance date. Critical for financial exposure tracking and contract administration.

Document reference number for the Operations and Maintenance (O&M) manual submitted as part of the handover package. Confirms that O&M documentation has been provided to the client as a condition of handover.

The submittal identifier assigned to the handover certificate package in Procore Construction Management. Enables cross-system traceability between the data lakehouse and the operational construction management platform.
The date by which all outstanding punch list items are contractually required to be resolved and closed. Agreed between contractor and client at the time of handover.

Number of punch list items classified as critical or Category A at the time of handover, representing items that must be resolved before or immediately after handover. Distinct from total punch list count.

description: Total number of outstanding punch list items (minor defects, incomplete works, or non-conformances) recorded at the time of handover certificate issuance. A key indicator of completion quality at handover.

Narrative description of the reason(s) for client rejection of the handover certificate, if applicable. Populated when certificate_status is 'rejected'. Supports root cause analysis and resubmission planning.

description: Indicates whether the issuance of this handover certificate triggers eligibility for release of contractual retention monies held by the client. Typically, substantial completion or final handover certificates trigger partial or full retention release.

description: Sequential revision number of the handover certificate, incremented each time the certificate is revised and reissued following client rejection or scope amendment. Revision 0 represents the original issue.

description: Narrative description of the works, systems, or areas being handed over under this certificate. Defines the physical and functional boundary of the handover including building sections, infrastructure systems, or MEP (Mechanical, Electrical, and Plumbing) systems.

reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Project Scheduling; PMI PMBOK WBS Standard

description: Formal criteria that must be satisfied for the milestone to be considered achieved and accepted by the client or engineer. May reference ITP (Inspection and Test Plan) requirements, regulatory sign-offs, or contractual deliverable submissions.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems; FIDIC Clause 10 Employer's Taking Over; ITP Standards

description: The date on which the milestone was formally achieved and accepted. Populated only upon milestone completion and sign-off. Used for schedule variance calculation, contract administration, and LD (Liquidated Damages) trigger assessment.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Progress Tracking; FIDIC Contract Administration; Procore Construction Management

description: Approved contract baseline date for the milestone, which may differ from the original planned date if a formal baseline revision or approved EOT (Extension of Time) has been incorporated. Used as the contractual reference for LD (Liquidated Damages) exposure calculation.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Baseline Management; FIDIC Clause 8; PMO Baseline Control

description: Percentage of work completed toward achieving this milestone, expressed as a value between 0.00 and 100.00. Updated during progress reporting cycles. Used for EVM (Earned Value Management) percent-complete calculations and schedule performance tracking.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Progress Tracking; EVM ANSI/EIA-748; Procore Construction Management

description: Timestamp when this milestone record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for data governance and lineage tracking in the Databricks lakehouse Silver layer.
reference: GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity; ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems; Data Governance Standards

description: The project delivery model under which this milestone is defined and tracked. Determines the contractual framework and milestone obligation structure. EPC = Engineering Procurement Construction; DB = Design-Build; DBB = Design-Bid-Build; PPP = Public-Private Partnership; BOT = Build-Operate-Transfer; GMP = Guaranteed Maximum Price.
reference: FIDIC Conditions of Contract; PMO Governance Framework

description: Total number of calendar days of approved Extension of Time (EOT) granted for this milestone under the contract. Reflects cumulative approved EOT claims that have adjusted the baseline date. Zero if no EOT has been granted.
reference: FIDIC Clause 8.4 Extension of Time for Completion; Contract Administration

description: Reference number of the approved EOT (Extension of Time) claim or contract amendment that adjusted the baseline date for this milestone. Used for contract administration audit trail and dispute resolution.
reference: FIDIC Clause 8.4 Extension of Time; Aconex Document Management; Contract Administration

description: Current best estimate of the date on which the milestone will be achieved, updated during schedule reviews and progress reporting cycles. Reflects schedule recovery plans, approved EOT (Extension of Time) claims, and current site conditions.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Progress Tracking; EVM (Earned Value Management) Schedule Forecasting

business_glossary_term: Health Safety Environment (HSE) Clearance Required Flag
description: Indicates whether HSE (Health, Safety and Environment) regulatory clearance or permit is a prerequisite for achieving this milestone (e.g., commissioning milestones requiring OSHA or EPA sign-off). True = HSE clearance must be obtained before milestone can be marked complete.
reference: OSHA Occupational Safety and Health Administration; ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety; EPA Environmental Protection Agency

description: Indicates whether this milestone is a contractually obligated key date as defined in the project contract (FIDIC, GMP, or bespoke agreement). True = contractual obligation with potential LD (Liquidated Damages) or payment consequences; False = internal PMO or programme milestone.
reference: FIDIC Conditions of Contract; Contract Administration Standards

description: Indicates whether this milestone lies on the Critical Path Method (CPM) critical path of the project schedule. True = any delay to this milestone directly delays the project completion date; False = milestone has float and is not on the critical path.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 CPM Scheduling; PMI PMBOK Schedule Management

description: Indicates whether missing this milestone triggers Liquidated Damages (LD) as specified in the contract. True = LD clause applies upon delay; False = no LD exposure for this milestone. Critical for contract risk management and financial exposure reporting.
reference: FIDIC Clause 8.7 Delay Damages; Contract Administration; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting

description: Indicates whether achievement of this milestone triggers a contractual payment event or progress payment certificate under the contract payment schedule. True = milestone completion initiates a payment claim or invoice; False = no direct payment linkage.
reference: FIDIC Clause 14 Contract Price and Payment; SAP S/4HANA Accounts Receivable

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the Liquidated Damages (LD) daily rate (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, AED). Ensures correct financial exposure reporting in multi-currency international EPC projects.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards

description: The contractually agreed daily monetary rate of Liquidated Damages (LD) applicable if this milestone is not achieved by the baseline date. Expressed in the project contract currency. Populated only when is_ld_trigger = True. Sensitive commercial data.
reference: FIDIC Clause 8.7 Delay Damages; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards

description: Indicates whether this milestone is associated with LEED (Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design) certification requirements or green building compliance deliverables. True = milestone contributes to LEED certification pathway.
reference: LEED U.S. Green Building Council; ISO 14001 Environmental Management Systems

description: Functional category grouping the milestone within the EPC (Engineering, Procurement, and Construction) project lifecycle phase. Supports schedule performance analysis by phase and PMO reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: design|procurement|construction|commissioning|handover|safety|financial|administrative — promote to reference product]
reference: EPC Project Lifecycle Framework; Oracle Primavera P6 Project Scheduling

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the milestone within the project schedule, as assigned in Oracle Primavera P6 (e.g., MS-NTP001, MS-MC001). Used for cross-system referencing and contract administration.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Project Scheduling; FIDIC Contract Administration

description: Human-readable name of the milestone describing the key project event or deliverable (e.g., 'Notice to Proceed (NTP)', 'Mechanical Completion', 'Substantial Completion', 'Final Handover'). Used in schedule reports and contract documents.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Project Scheduling; FIDIC Conditions of Contract

description: Current lifecycle state of the milestone tracking its progress toward completion. 'not_started' = work not yet begun; 'in_progress' = activities underway; 'completed' = milestone achieved; 'overdue' = past planned date without completion; 'waived' = formally waived by contract amendment; 'deferred' = postponed with approved EOT (Extension of Time).
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Progress Tracking; FIDIC Clause 8 Extension of Time

description: Classification of the milestone by its origin and obligation level. 'contractual' = bound by contract terms (FIDIC/GMP); 'internal' = PMO governance milestone; 'regulatory' = required by OSHA/EPA/IBC; 'client' = client-driven key date; 'financial' = triggers payment or LD exposure.
reference: FIDIC Conditions of Contract; PMO Governance Framework; OSHA Regulatory Requirements

description: Number of calendar days in advance of the milestone date that formal notification must be issued to the client or engineer, as required by the contract. Applicable when notification_required = True. Supports contract compliance monitoring.
reference: FIDIC Conditions of Contract; Contract Administration Standards

description: Indicates whether formal written notification to the client or engineer is contractually required prior to or upon achievement of this milestone. True = notification obligation exists per contract; False = no formal notification required.
reference: FIDIC Clause 8 Commencement, Delays and Suspension; Contract Administration

description: Contractual payment amount due upon achievement of this milestone, expressed in the project contract currency. Applicable only when is_payment_trigger = True. Used for cash flow forecasting and contract financial management.
reference: FIDIC Clause 14 Contract Price and Payment; IFRS/GAAP Revenue Recognition; SAP S/4HANA Finance

description: Original baseline date on which the milestone was scheduled to be achieved, as established at project kick-off or NTP (Notice to Proceed). Serves as the schedule baseline reference for variance analysis and SPI (Schedule Performance Index) calculation.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Baseline Management; FIDIC Conditions of Contract

description: The corresponding milestone or schedule item identifier in Procore Construction Management platform. Supports cross-system reconciliation between the scheduling system of record (Primavera P6) and the construction management platform (Procore).
reference: Procore Construction Management; Data Integration Standards

description: Detailed narrative description of the milestone, including the scope of work, acceptance criteria, and deliverables required to formally achieve the milestone. Sourced from the contract schedule or project execution plan.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Project Scheduling; FIDIC Conditions of Contract

description: Free-text field for project team notes, status commentary, risk flags, or contextual information related to the milestone. Used during progress review meetings and PMO reporting to capture qualitative schedule narrative.
reference: PMO Governance Framework; Oracle Primavera P6 Project Scheduling

description: The contracting party primarily responsible for achieving this milestone. Distinguishes between the General Contractor (GC), client, subcontractor, engineer/designer, Joint Venture (JV) partner, or regulatory authority. Critical for contract administration and risk allocation.
reference: FIDIC Conditions of Contract; Contract Administration Standards

description: Number of calendar days by which the milestone is ahead of (negative) or behind (positive) the baseline date. Calculated as forecast_date minus baseline_date for open milestones, or actual_date minus baseline_date for completed milestones. Key input for SPI (Schedule Performance Index) reporting.
reference: EVM (Earned Value Management) ANSI/EIA-748; Oracle Primavera P6 Schedule Analysis

description: Reference number of the formal sign-off document, completion certificate, or taking-over certificate issued upon milestone achievement (e.g., Aconex document number, Procore submittal reference). Provides audit trail for contract administration and handover documentation.
reference: FIDIC Clause 10 Taking Over Certificate; Aconex Document Management; Procore Construction Management

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this milestone record was ingested into the Databricks lakehouse Silver layer. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and master data management across the construction technology stack.
reference: Data Governance Standards; Databricks Lakehouse Architecture

description: Total schedule float in calendar days available for this milestone before it impacts the project completion date, as calculated by the CPM (Critical Path Method) schedule engine in Oracle Primavera P6. Zero or negative float indicates critical or super-critical status.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 CPM Scheduling; ANSI/EIA-748 EVM Standard

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this milestone record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change detection, incremental data loading, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks lakehouse Silver layer.
reference: GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity; ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems; Data Governance Standards

description: Primary key for site
Identifier of the project to which the site belongs.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Construction sites are assigned to cost centers for site-level cost tracking, overhead allocation, and financial reporting. The site.cost_center_code is a denormalized reference. A proper FK enables s

Date when construction was actually completed.
Cumulative actual expenditure incurred at the site.
Primary street address of the site.
Secondary address information (suite, building, etc.).
Total land area of the site in square meters.
Approved budget for the site in the project currency.
City where the site is located.
ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code where the site resides.

ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values.
Site elevation above sea level in meters.
Environmental compliance zone applicable to the site.
Observed condition of the ground (e.g., rocky, soft, contaminated).
Date when the site insurance coverage expires.
Policy number of the insurance covering the site.
Date of the most recent health, safety, or regulatory inspection.
Geographic latitude of the site in decimal degrees.
Geographic longitude of the site in decimal degrees.
Email address of the site manager for official communications.
