Primary key for cost_center

Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: Required for Cost Center Allocation Report per client, enabling profitability analysis by linking each cost center to the client account it serves.

Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: cost_center stores company_code as a plain STRING column. In SAP controlling, every cost center belongs to a specific company code (legal entity). Adding company_code_id FK normalizes this reference a

Identifier of the parent cost center in the organizational hierarchy. Enables roll-up reporting for regional, divisional, and corporate-level P&L (Profit and Loss) and EBITDA (Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization) analysis. Nullable for top-level cost centers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA Hierarchy; Management Reporting Standards

description: Identifier of the profit center to which this cost center is assigned. Profit centers aggregate revenue and costs for internal P&L (Profit and Loss) reporting; cost centers roll up into profit centers for EBITDA (Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization) calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA (Profit Center Accounting); Management Accounting Standards

description: The methodology used to allocate or distribute costs from this cost center to other cost objects (projects, WBS (Work Breakdown Structure) elements, or profit centers). Direct charge posts costs explicitly; activity-based uses cost drivers; headcount and square footage are common overhead allocation bases; revenue-based allocates proportionally to project revenue; none indicates no allocation (terminal cost center).
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA Allocation Cycles; Management Accounting Best Practices

description: The approved annual or period budget allocated to this cost center. Used for budget vs. actual variance reporting, CPI (Cost Performance Index) calculation in EVM (Earned Value Management), and financial forecasting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA Budget Management; IFRS/GAAP Budgeting Standards

description: The time horizon for which the budget amount is defined. Annual budgets align with fiscal year planning; quarterly and monthly budgets support rolling forecasts; project lifecycle budgets span the duration of a construction project from NTP (Notice to Proceed) to handover.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA Budget Management; Internal Financial Planning Standards

description: The business segment or division to which this cost center is assigned (e.g., Infrastructure, Energy, Commercial, Residential). Used for segment reporting under IFRS 8 and internal divisional P&L (Profit and Loss) analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Business Area; IFRS 8 Operating Segments

description: The SAP S/4HANA Controlling (CO) area to which this cost center belongs. Controlling areas represent organizational units for internal management accounting and can span multiple company codes. Used for cross-company cost center reporting and consolidation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Controlling Area; Management Accounting Framework

description: Accounting classification of the cost center for financial treatment. Direct centers charge costs to specific projects or WBS (Work Breakdown Structure) elements; indirect centers allocate costs via overhead rates; overhead centers represent corporate G&A (General and Administrative); capitalized centers accumulate costs for asset creation; expensed centers flow directly to P&L (Profit and Loss).
reference: IFRS/GAAP Cost Accounting Standards; SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA

description: The externally-known alphanumeric code identifying the cost center in financial systems and reports. Used in SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA (Cost Center Accounting) for posting and reporting. Typically follows organizational coding standards (e.g., project-based, regional, functional).
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA; IFRS/GAAP Chart of Accounts Standards

description: Extended free-text description providing additional context about the cost center's purpose, scope, and responsibilities. Used for documentation, audit trails, and onboarding new finance team members.

A user-defined grouping or classification for aggregating related cost centers in reports (e.g., all regional offices, all project cost centers for a specific client, all MEP (Mechanical Electrical and Plumbing) cost centers). Used for flexible reporting hierarchies beyond the standard organizational structure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA Cost Center Groups; Internal Reporting Standards

description: The full descriptive name of the cost center, used for human-readable identification in financial reports, dashboards, and P&L (Profit and Loss) statements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA; Internal Financial Reporting Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the cost center. Active centers accept postings; inactive centers are temporarily disabled; planned centers are approved but not yet operational; closed centers are archived and no longer accept transactions; suspended centers are on hold pending review or restructuring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA; Internal Financial Controls

description: Classification of the cost center by its primary business function. Project cost centers track job-specific costs; regional offices track geographic operations; corporate overhead captures non-project administrative costs; shared services represent centralized functions (IT, HR, Finance); support functions include PMO (Project Management Office), procurement, and QA/QC (Quality Assurance/Quality Control); operational centers track site-level or equipment-based activities.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA; Management Accounting Best Practices

description: The primary cost element group or cost category that this cost center typically incurs (e.g., labor, materials, equipment, subcontractor, overhead). Used for cost structure analysis and variance reporting by cost type.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Element Accounting; Internal Cost Classification

description: The username or identifier of the system user who created this cost center record. Used for accountability, audit trails, and access control reviews.
reference: SAP S/4HANA User Audit; Internal Data Governance Policy

description: The date and time when this cost center record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails, data lineage tracking, and compliance with financial record-keeping regulations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail; IFRS/GAAP Record Retention Standards

description: The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which this cost center's budget and actuals are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Used for multi-currency consolidation and foreign exchange variance analysis.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA Currency Management

description: The primary functional discipline or department this cost center supports. Used for cross-project functional cost analysis, overhead allocation, and shared-service chargeback models. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: engineering|procurement|construction|quality|safety|finance|hr|it|legal|admin — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA Functional Area; Internal Cost Allocation Framework

description: The depth of this cost center in the organizational hierarchy tree. Level 1 represents corporate or top-level centers; higher numbers represent nested project, site, or functional centers. Used for drill-down reporting and aggregation logic.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA Hierarchy; Internal Reporting Framework

description: Indicates whether costs incurred in this cost center are billable to external clients or internal projects. True for project cost centers with reimbursable contracts; false for corporate overhead and non-billable support functions. Used for revenue recognition and progress billing logic.
reference: IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; Internal Billing Policy

description: The username or identifier of the system user who last modified this cost center record. Used for change accountability, audit trails, and data quality investigations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA User Audit; Internal Data Governance Policy

description: The date and time when this cost center record was last updated. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and data quality monitoring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail; IFRS/GAAP Record Retention Standards

description: Indicates whether this cost center is locked for new postings. True when the cost center is under audit, pending closure, or frozen for period-end reporting. Used to enforce financial controls and prevent unauthorized transactions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA Posting Controls; Internal Financial Controls

description: The standard overhead rate (as a percentage) applied to direct costs posted to this cost center. Used for indirect cost allocation, GMP (Guaranteed Maximum Price) contract pricing, and full absorption costing. Nullable for cost centers that do not apply overhead rates.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA Overhead Calculation; IFRS/GAAP Cost Accounting

description: Three-letter code representing the geographic region or country where this cost center operates (e.g., USA, GBR, ARE). Used for regional P&L (Profit and Loss) reporting, tax jurisdiction mapping, and geographic segment analysis.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; Internal Geographic Segmentation

description: The physical site, office, or facility location associated with this cost center. Used for site-level cost tracking, field operations reporting, and HSE (Health Safety and Environment) cost allocation.
reference: Internal Site Master Data; GIS (Geographic Information System) Integration

description: The date from which this cost center becomes effective and can accept financial postings. Used for time-dependent cost center master data and period-based reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA; IFRS/GAAP Period Accounting

description: The date until which this cost center remains effective. Nullable for open-ended cost centers. Used to manage cost center lifecycle, project closeout, and organizational restructuring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA; IFRS/GAAP Period Accounting

description: Primary key for cost_code
FK to finance.cost_center

Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether cost postings to this code require additional approval workflow beyond standard posting authorization. Used for high-risk or high-value cost categories.
reference: Internal Control Standards; Project Cost Control Procedures

description: Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether this cost code can have budget allocated to it. Typically True for leaf-level codes and False for parent/rollup codes.
reference: Project Budget Management Standards; SAP S/4HANA Budget Allocation Rules

description: Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether costs posted to this code should be capitalized as assets on the balance sheet rather than expensed. Critical for Engineering Procurement and Construction (EPC) projects and asset-intensive developments.
reference: IFRS/GAAP Capitalization Standards; IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment

description: The primary classification of the cost code into major expenditure categories. Used for high-level financial reporting and Profit and Loss (P&L) analysis.
reference: Construction Accounting Standards; IFRS/GAAP Cost Classification

description: The unique alphanumeric cost code identifier used across all project accounting systems. This is the externally-known business identifier used in SAP S/4HANA Project Systems and Viewpoint Vista for budget allocation and actual cost posting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Project Systems Cost Element Master; Viewpoint Vista Cost Code Structure

description: Detailed description of the cost code explaining the scope of work, materials, or services covered by this classification. Provides additional context beyond the name for proper cost allocation.
reference: Construction Accounting Standards; Project Cost Management Guidelines

description: The hierarchical level of this cost code within the Cost Breakdown Structure (CBS). Typically ranges from 1 (top-level category) to 4 (detailed line item). Level 1 might be 'Labor', Level 2 'Structural Labor', Level 3 'Steel Erection Labor'.
reference: Cost Breakdown Structure (CBS) Standards; Project Cost Management Guidelines

description: The full descriptive name of the cost code providing human-readable identification of the cost category (e.g., 'Structural Steel Erection', 'Concrete Formwork Labor', 'Electrical Conduit Installation').
reference: Construction Accounting Standards; Viewpoint Vista Cost Code Master

description: Current lifecycle status of the cost code. Active codes are available for budget allocation and cost posting. Inactive codes are retained for historical reporting but cannot be used for new transactions. Deprecated codes have been superseded by newer classifications.
reference: Master Data Management Standards; SAP S/4HANA Cost Element Status Management

description: Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether actual costs can be posted directly to this cost code. Typically True for detailed codes and False for summary/rollup codes.
reference: Construction Accounting Standards; Viewpoint Vista Cost Posting Rules

description: Classification indicating whether the cost is directly attributable to a specific project deliverable (direct) or allocated across multiple activities (indirect/overhead). Critical for job costing and Earned Value Management (EVM) calculations.
reference: Construction Accounting Standards; EVM Guidelines; Job Costing Best Practices

description: The date and time when this cost code record was first created in the system. Part of the standard audit trail for master data management.
reference: Data Governance Standards; ISO 8601 Timestamp Format

description: The two-digit CSI MasterFormat division number (00-49) that this cost code maps to. Provides standardized alignment with construction specifications and Bill of Quantities (BOQ) structures.
reference: CSI MasterFormat 2020; Construction Specifications Institute Standards

description: The engineering or construction discipline associated with this cost code (e.g., 'CIVIL', 'STRUCT', 'MECH', 'ELEC', 'INST' for Mechanical Electrical Plumbing (MEP) disciplines). Used for discipline-specific cost tracking and resource planning.
reference: Engineering Discipline Classification Standards; Construction Specification Institute (CSI) MasterFormat

description: The date after which this cost code is no longer active for new transactions. Null for currently active codes. Used for cost code retirement and historical reporting.
reference: Master Data Management Standards; Temporal Data Management Guidelines

description: The date from which this cost code becomes active and available for use in budget allocation and cost posting. Supports time-based cost code structures and phased project implementations.
reference: Master Data Management Standards; Temporal Data Management Guidelines

description: Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether costs posted to this code are included in Earned Value Management (EVM) calculations including Cost Performance Index (CPI) and Schedule Performance Index (SPI) metrics.
reference: EVM Standards (ANSI/EIA-748); Project Cost Control Guidelines

description: The unique identifier for this cost code in the source system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA Cost Element number, Viewpoint Vista Cost Code ID). Used for cross-system reconciliation and integration.
reference: Enterprise Integration Standards; System Integration Guidelines

description: The General Ledger (GL) account code in SAP S/4HANA Finance that this cost code maps to for financial statement reporting. Enables integration between project costing and corporate accounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Finance GL Master; IFRS/GAAP Chart of Accounts Standards

description: The user ID or name of the person who last modified this cost code record. Used for change tracking and data governance.
reference: Data Governance Standards; Change Management Standards

description: The date and time when this cost code record was last modified. Part of the standard audit trail for master data management and change tracking.
reference: Data Governance Standards; ISO 8601 Timestamp Format

description: Free-text field for additional notes, usage guidelines, or special instructions related to this cost code. Provides context for proper application and interpretation.
The cost code of the parent category in the hierarchical Cost Breakdown Structure (CBS). Null for top-level cost codes. Enables roll-up reporting and hierarchical budget analysis.
reference: Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) Standards; Cost Breakdown Structure (CBS) Guidelines

description: The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the standard rate (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP'). Required for multi-currency projects and international joint ventures.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS/GAAP Multi-Currency Accounting Standards

description: The accounting method used for revenue recognition associated with this cost code. Percentage of completion is the most common method for construction projects under IFRS 15 and ASC 606.
reference: IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers; ASC 606 Revenue Recognition; Construction Accounting Standards

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this cost code was originally sourced or is mastered. Supports multi-system integration and data lineage tracking.
reference: Enterprise Integration Standards; Master Data Management Guidelines

description: The standard cost rate per unit of measure for this cost code. Used for budget estimation, variance analysis, and Earned Value Management (EVM) calculations. May represent labor rate per hour, material cost per unit, or equipment rate per day.
reference: Construction Cost Estimating Standards; EVM Baseline Guidelines

description: Code indicating the tax treatment for costs posted to this cost code (e.g., 'TAXABLE', 'EXEMPT', 'ZERO_RATED'). Used for Value Added Tax (VAT) and sales tax compliance in multi-jurisdiction projects.
reference: Tax Compliance Standards; VAT/GST Regulations; IFRS/GAAP Tax Accounting

description: The standard unit of measure for quantity tracking associated with this cost code (e.g., 'hours' for labor, 'cubic yards' for concrete, 'linear feet' for piping, 'each' for equipment). Used for productivity analysis and Material Take-Off (MTO) reconciliation.
reference: Construction Industry Standard Units; SAP S/4HANA Materials Management UOM Master

description: Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether this cost code can be directly linked to Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) elements in Oracle Primavera P6 and SAP S/4HANA Project Systems for integrated schedule and cost management.
reference: WBS Integration Standards; Oracle Primavera P6 Cost Integration Guidelines

description: The user ID or name of the person who created this cost code record in the system. Used for audit trail and data governance.
reference: Data Governance Standards; Audit Trail Requirements

description: Unique identifier for the job cost transaction record. Primary key for this entity.
Reference to the equipment asset used or charged in this transaction. Applicable for equipment usage and rental cost transactions.
Reference to the construction project to which this cost transaction is posted.

Foreign key linking to contract.contract_change_order. Business justification: Approved contract change orders authorize additional cost expenditure. Job cost transactions for change-order scope must reference the authorizing change order for earned value management, cost-to-com
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Job cost transactions are charged to a cost center; FK enforces valid cost center reference.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: job_cost_transaction currently stores cost_code as a plain STRING column, which is a denormalization. Every job cost transaction must be classified against the cost coding taxonomy master (cost_code)

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: job_cost_transaction stores gl_account_code as a plain STRING, which is a denormalization of the GL account master. Every cost transaction must post to a specific GL account for double-entry accountin

Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: In construction job costing, goods receipts trigger actual cost postings. Cost controllers must trace every job cost transaction back to its source GR document for three-way match validation and cost

Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line. Business justification: Construction job cost transactions must be traceable to specific PO line items for earned value management and cost-at-completion forecasting. job_cost_transaction.purchase_order_number is a plain-tex

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Environmental levies, compliance fees, and permit costs are job cost transactions tied to specific regulatory obligations. Construction cost controllers track which regulatory obligation drove each co
Reference to the original job cost transaction that this record reverses or corrects. Null for original transactions.

Foreign key linking to contract.subcontract. Business justification: Job cost transactions for subcontractor expenditure must reference the authorizing subcontract for committed cost tracking, subcontract budget vs. actual analysis, and cost-to-complete forecasting. Co

Foreign key linking to bid.trade_package. Business justification: Job cost transactions for subcontractor work are posted against trade packages — enabling committed-cost vs. actual-cost variance reporting by trade package. Construction cost controllers require this
Reference to the vendor or subcontractor who provided the goods or services for this transaction. Applicable for material, equipment rental, and subcontractor costs.

Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_invoice. Business justification: Vendor invoice processing in construction generates job cost transactions (actual cost postings). Cost accountants must trace job cost entries back to source vendor invoices for audit and dispute reso
Reference to the WBS element within the project structure against which this cost is charged. Enables hierarchical cost rollup and EVM integration.
The approval workflow status of this cost transaction: pending (awaiting review), approved (validated and committed), or rejected (not accepted).
The name or identifier of the person who approved this cost transaction. Used for audit trail and accountability.
The date and time when this cost transaction was approved. Used for audit trail and workflow tracking.
The total cost converted to the project or corporate base currency for consolidated financial reporting and P&L analysis.
Indicates whether this cost is billable to the client (true) or non-billable internal cost (false). Used for progress billing and revenue recognition.
Indicates whether this cost has been included in a client invoice (true) or remains unbilled (false). Used for work-in-progress and revenue recognition tracking.
High-level classification of the cost transaction type: labor, material, equipment, subcontractor, overhead, or indirect costs.
The date and time when this cost transaction record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data governance.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency project accounting.
The exchange rate applied to convert the transaction amount to the project base currency. Null if transaction is in base currency.
The fiscal period (e.g., 2024-Q1, 2024-03) to which this transaction is assigned for financial reporting and period close.
Free-text description of the cost transaction providing additional context about the work performed, materials used, or services rendered.
The user or system that last modified this cost transaction record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
The date and time when this cost transaction record was last modified. Used for audit trail and data governance.
Additional free-text notes or comments about this cost transaction, including justifications, clarifications, or special handling instructions.
The date when the transaction was posted to the financial system. May differ from transaction_date due to approval or processing delays.
The quantity of the resource consumed or work performed (e.g., labor hours, material units, equipment hours). Used for unit cost analysis.
Indicates whether this transaction is a reversal or correction of a previously posted cost (true) or an original transaction (false).
The operational system from which this cost transaction originated (e.g., Viewpoint Vista, SAP S/4HANA, HCSS HeavyJob). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
The unique transaction identifier in the source system. Enables traceability back to the originating record for audit and reconciliation.
The total cost amount for this transaction (quantity × unit_cost). Represents the actual cost posted to the project for EVM ACWP and CPI calculations.
The business date when the cost was incurred or the work was performed. Used for period-based cost reporting and EVM actual cost (ACWP) calculations.
Business-facing unique transaction identifier or document number from the source system (e.g., timesheet number, invoice number, receipt number).
Current lifecycle status of the cost transaction: draft (pending approval), posted (committed to ledger), approved (validated), reversed (corrected), or cancelled.
The cost per unit of measure for this transaction. Used to calculate total cost and for rate variance analysis.
The unit in which the quantity is measured (e.g., hours, cubic meters, tons, each). Aligns with industry standard UOM codes.
The user or system that created this cost transaction record. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Unique identifier for the project budget record. Primary key for this entity.
Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Needed to tie budget allocations directly to the governing contract, enabling cost control and variance reporting per agreement.
Reference to the construction project this budget belongs to. Links to the master project record.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Project budgets are associated with a cost center; replace the free‑text cost_center column with a foreign key.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Project budgets reference a cost code; replace the free‑text cost_code column with a foreign key to the cost_code master.

Foreign key linking to project.phase. Business justification: Construction project budgets are structured by phase (design, procurement, construction, commissioning). Phase-level budgets enable gate-review financial approvals and phase-level EVM reporting. Linki
Reference to the prior version of this budget record if this is a revision. Enables historical tracking and variance analysis across budget versions.

Foreign key linking to project.project_baseline. Business justification: Project baselines establish the approved cost baseline that project_budget records are created from. In construction EVM, the project_budget must reference the approved baseline for variance-at-comple

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Construction project budgets include dedicated line items for regulatory compliance costs (environmental fees, permit costs, statutory levies). Budget owners allocate funds against specific regulatory
Total actual costs incurred to date against this budget. Includes invoiced amounts and accrued costs. Used for EVM Actual Cost of Work Performed (ACWP).
Date when this budget record was formally approved by authorized stakeholders. Triggers budget activation and commitment authority.
Name or identifier of the person who approved this budget. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Cumulative sum of all approved change order adjustments to the budget. Includes client-approved scope changes and contract modifications.
Date when this budget was approved and baselined. Establishes the performance measurement baseline for EVM.
Unique business identifier for the budget record. Used for external reporting and cross-system reference.
Descriptive name of the budget line item or WBS budget element. Human-readable identifier for reporting.
Free-text notes and comments about this budget record. Captures assumptions, constraints, change justifications, and other contextual information.
Name or identifier of the project manager or cost manager responsible for managing this budget. Accountable for budget performance.
End date of the budget period this record applies to. Defines the time window for budget consumption and variance analysis.
Start date of the budget period this record applies to. Used for time-phased budget tracking and cash flow forecasting.
Current lifecycle status of the budget record. Approved budgets are baselined; active budgets are in use; frozen budgets are locked for change control.
Classification of the budget record. Contract budget represents client-approved amounts; internal budget represents target cost; contingency and management reserve represent risk buffers.
Unit of measure for quantity-based budgets (e.g., cubic meters for concrete, linear meters for piping, hours for labor). Used for unit rate calculations.
Difference between current approved budget and actual costs. Positive values indicate under-budget; negative values indicate over-budget.
Planned quantity of work or materials for this budget line. Used with unit rates to calculate total budget amounts.
Total value of purchase orders and subcontracts issued against this budget. Represents contractual obligations not yet invoiced.
Budget allocation for identified risks and known-unknowns. Managed by the project manager and released through change control process.
Reference to the specific contract line item or BOQ item this budget corresponds to. Links budget to contractual scope and payment terms.
Date and time when this budget record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this budget record. Supports multi-currency project accounting.
The current total approved budget including original budget plus all approved change orders. This is the active baseline for Earned Value Management (EVM) calculations.
Projected total cost at project completion based on current performance trends. Used for variance analysis and early warning of budget overruns.
Source of funding for this budget (e.g., client payment, internal investment, joint venture contribution, government grant). Used for cash flow and financial reporting.
Indicates whether this budget record is currently active and available for cost posting. Inactive budgets are closed or superseded.
Indicates whether this budget record represents the approved performance measurement baseline for EVM. True for baseline budgets used in BCWS calculations.
Date and time when this budget record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change for audit and synchronization purposes.
Budget allocation for unknown-unknowns and unidentified risks. Held at executive level and not part of the performance measurement baseline.
The initial approved budget amount at project baseline. Represents the starting contract value or internal target cost before any change orders.
Sequential version number for this budget record. Incremented with each approved change to maintain audit trail of budget evolution.
Cost per unit of measure for this budget item. Multiplied by budgeted quantity to derive total budget amount.

Primary key for gl_account
Reference to the company code (legal entity) to which this account belongs. Supports multi-entity chart of accounts.
High-level classification indicating whether the account appears on the balance sheet, Profit and Loss (P&L) statement, or is a statistical (non-financial) account.
Grouping code used to aggregate accounts for reporting and analysis (e.g., 'Direct Construction Costs', 'Overhead', 'Operating Expenses', 'Current Assets').
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Account Group, IFRS/GAAP

description: The full descriptive name of the account (e.g., 'Construction Materials - Direct Costs', 'Accounts Payable - Trade', 'Revenue - EPC Contracts').
The externally-known unique account number in the chart of accounts. Typically a 4-10 digit numeric code used in all financial postings and reports.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Chart of Accounts, IFRS/GAAP

description: Abbreviated name or label for the account used in condensed reports and user interfaces.
Current lifecycle status of the account: active (in use), blocked (no new postings), closed (archived), or pending approval (awaiting activation).
The fundamental financial statement category: asset, liability, equity, revenue, or expense. Determines balance sheet vs. Profit and Loss (P&L) classification.
Alternative or legacy account number used for cross-reference, migration mapping, or external reporting (e.g., statutory chart of accounts).
Detailed balance sheet line item classification (e.g., 'Current Assets - Cash', 'Non-Current Liabilities - Long-Term Debt', 'Equity - Retained Earnings'). Null for P&L accounts.
reference: IFRS IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements, GAAP

description: Classification for cash flow statement reporting: operating activities, investing activities, financing activities, or not applicable for non-cash accounts.
reference: IFRS IAS 7 Statement of Cash Flows, GAAP ASC 230

description: The corresponding account number in the group consolidation chart of accounts. Used for mapping local entity accounts to group reporting structure.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements, SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting

description: Flag indicating whether a cost center must be specified when posting to this account. Enforces cost allocation discipline.
Classification of the cost element: primary (direct posting from FI), secondary (internal allocation), revenue, or not applicable if not a cost element.
Flag indicating whether this account is also a cost element in the controlling (CO) module, enabling integration with project costing and Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) budgets.
Date and time when this account master record was first created in the system.
Indicates the currency perspective for this account: local (entity currency), group (consolidation currency), transaction (original currency), or project (project-specific currency).
reference: IFRS IAS 21 The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates, SAP S/4HANA Currency Management

description: Configuration key that controls which fields are required, optional, or suppressed when posting to this account (e.g., cost center, WBS, profit center).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Field Status Variant

description: Business function or division associated with the account (e.g., 'Project Operations', 'Corporate Overhead', 'Procurement', 'MEP Services'). Used for segment reporting.
reference: IFRS 8 Operating Segments, SAP S/4HANA Functional Area

description: Flag indicating whether individual line items (journal entries) are stored and can be displayed for this account. Required for detailed transaction analysis.
User ID or name of the person who last modified this account master record.
Date and time when this account master record was last modified.
Flag indicating whether open item management is active for this account (e.g., for clearing transactions like AP/AR, advances, or accruals).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Open Item Management

description: Indicates the level at which budgeting and planning are performed for this account (e.g., 'Corporate', 'Division', 'Project', 'Cost Center').
Flag indicating whether direct postings are allowed to this account. False for summary/control accounts that only receive automatic postings.

Detailed Profit and Loss (P&L) line item classification (e.g., 'Revenue - Construction Contracts', 'Cost of Sales - Direct Labor', 'Operating Expenses - General & Administrative'). Null for balance sheet accounts.
reference: IFRS IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements, GAAP

description: Flag indicating whether a profit center must be specified when posting to this account. Supports segment profitability analysis.
Flag indicating whether this is a reconciliation account (subledger control account) for Accounts Payable (AP), Accounts Receivable (AR), or asset accounting.
Flag indicating whether this is the retained earnings account used for year-end profit/loss transfer and equity rollforward.
reference: IFRS IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements, GAAP

description: Default sort key used for line item display and reporting (e.g., posting date, document number, reference). Controls how transactions are ordered.
Tax classification code indicating how this account is treated for tax purposes (e.g., 'Input Tax', 'Output Tax', 'Non-Taxable', 'Exempt'). Used for VAT/GST reporting.
reference: Local Tax Regulations, SAP S/4HANA Tax Configuration

description: Tolerance group code that defines permissible payment differences, exchange rate variances, and rounding tolerances for this account.
Flag indicating whether a trading partner (intercompany entity) must be specified when posting to this account. Used for intercompany eliminations.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements, SAP S/4HANA Intercompany Accounting

description: The date from which this account is valid and available for posting. Supports time-dependent chart of accounts changes.
The date until which this account is valid. Null for accounts with no planned end date. Used for account retirement and chart of accounts evolution.

business_glossary_term: Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) Element Required Indicator
description: Flag indicating whether a Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) element must be specified when posting to this account. Ensures project-level cost tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PS-Project System, PMI PMBOK WBS Standards

description: User ID or name of the person who created this account master record in the system.
Unique identifier for the journal entry document. Primary key for the journal entry record.

Foreign key reference to the construction project to which this journal entry is allocated. Enables project-level P&L and EBITDA reporting.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: journal_entry stores cost_center_code as a plain STRING. The journal entry header should reference the cost_center master via FK for proper cost center reporting and controlling. journal_entry_line al

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: journal_entry stores cost_code as a plain STRING column. Journal entries in construction accounting must reference the cost coding taxonomy for project cost classification. Adding cost_code_id FK norm

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Journal entry posts to a specific GL account; linking to gl_account master eliminates code duplication and enables consistent account attributes.

Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Goods receipts in construction trigger inventory/GR-IR journal entries (debit stock/expense, credit GR-IR clearing). Finance teams must link GL postings to source GR documents for GR-IR account reconc

Foreign key linking to contract.ld_assessment. Business justification: Liquidated damages assessments trigger GL journal entries (LD revenue recognition or contractor payable deduction). Finance teams post JEs referencing the LD assessment for audit trail, dispute resolu

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: journal_entry stores profit_center_code as a plain STRING. In SAP S/4HANA-integrated construction accounting, journal entries are assigned to profit centers for P&L reporting. Adding profit_center_id

Foreign key linking to contract.contract_retention_ledger. Business justification: Each retention movement (withholding or release) in the contract retention ledger generates a GL journal entry. Finance teams post JEs for retention events and must reference the retention ledger entr

Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_invoice. Business justification: AP invoice processing in construction generates GL journal entries (debit expense/asset, credit AP). Auditors and finance teams must trace journal entries back to source vendor invoices for three-way
Free-form assignment field used for additional sorting, grouping, or reconciliation purposes (e.g., invoice number, payment reference).
The business area or division for cross-company code reporting and segment analysis.
The date and time when this journal entry record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and compliance.
The credit amount for this journal entry line in the document currency. Zero if this is a debit entry.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the journal entry amounts are denominated.
The debit amount for this journal entry line in the document currency. Zero if this is a credit entry.
The date on the source document or invoice that originated this journal entry. May differ from posting date for period-end adjustments.
The externally-known unique document number assigned to this journal entry in the general ledger system. Used for audit trail and reference in financial reports.
Classification of the journal entry document type. SA=Standard Accrual, AA=Asset Accounting, KR=Vendor Invoice, DR=Customer Invoice, AB=Accounting Document, RV=Reversal Document.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Document Type Configuration

description: Current lifecycle status of the journal entry. Draft=not yet posted, Posted=finalized in GL, Parked=saved but not posted, Reversed=reversed by another entry, Cancelled=voided.
The foreign exchange rate used to convert document currency amounts to local currency. Applicable for multi-currency transactions.
The type of exchange rate used for currency conversion. Spot=transaction date rate, Average=period average, Budget=budgeted rate, Closing=period-end rate.
The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year to which this journal entry is assigned. Typically 1-12 for monthly periods, or 13-16 for special closing periods.
The fiscal year to which this journal entry is assigned for financial reporting purposes.
Free-form descriptive text at the document header level explaining the purpose or context of the journal entry.
Flag indicating whether this journal entry represents an intercompany transaction requiring elimination during consolidation.
Free-form descriptive text at the line item level providing additional context for this specific posting.
The credit amount converted to the local currency of the company code for statutory reporting.
The debit amount converted to the local currency of the company code for statutory reporting.
The user ID or name of the person who last modified this journal entry document.
The date and time when this journal entry record was last modified in the system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
The percentage of completion used for revenue recognition entries under IFRS 15. Represents the stage of project completion at the time of this journal entry.
reference: IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers

description: The user ID or name of the person who posted this journal entry to the general ledger, finalizing the transaction.
The date and time when this journal entry was posted to the general ledger. Represents the moment the entry became final and immutable.
The date on which the journal entry is posted to the general ledger. This is the principal business event timestamp for financial period assignment and reporting.
Two-digit code that controls the type of posting (debit or credit) and the account type (GL, customer, vendor, asset). Standard SAP posting keys include 40=GL Debit, 50=GL Credit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Posting Key Configuration

description: External reference number or identifier linking this journal entry to source documents such as invoices, purchase orders, or contracts.

The method used to calculate percentage of completion for revenue recognition. Cost-to-cost=based on costs incurred, Units-delivered=based on deliverables, Milestones=based on project milestones, Time-elapsed=based on time.
reference: IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers

description: Flag indicating whether this journal entry is a reversal of a previous entry. True if this is a reversal document.
Code indicating the reason for reversal. 01=Error Correction, 02=Period-End Adjustment, 03=Accrual Reversal, 04=Reclassification, 05=Other.
The document number of the original journal entry that this entry reverses. Populated only when reversal_indicator is true.
The calculated tax amount for this journal entry line in the document currency.
The tax code applied to this journal entry line, determining the tax rate and tax account for automatic tax calculation.
The trading partner company code for intercompany transactions, used for consolidation and elimination entries.
The WBS element code linking this journal entry to a specific project phase or deliverable. Enables project-level financial tracking and EVM integration.
The user ID or name of the person who created this journal entry document in the system.
Unique identifier for the journal entry line item. Primary key for this entity representing one leg of a double-entry accounting transaction.
Reference to the client or customer associated with this line item, if applicable. Used for accounts receivable reconciliation and revenue recognition.
Reference to the construction project to which this financial transaction is allocated. Links financial data to project master data for job costing and project P&L.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6, SAP S/4HANA Project Systems

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Journal entry lines need to be allocated to a cost center; replace the existing cost_center_code string with a proper foreign key.
FK to finance.cost_code
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Journal entry line items reference GL accounts; FK provides authoritative account details.
Reference to the parent journal entry header that contains this line item. Links this line to the overall transaction batch and posting context.
FK to finance.profit_center
Reference to the vendor or subcontractor associated with this line item, if applicable. Used for accounts payable reconciliation and vendor spend analysis.
FK to project.wbs_element
The monetary amount of this journal entry line in the local reporting currency of the entity. Used for financial statement preparation and local statutory reporting.
The monetary amount of this journal entry line in the original transaction currency, before any currency conversion. Enables multi-currency accounting and foreign exchange analysis.
Additional reference field used for payment allocation, clearing, and reconciliation. Often contains check numbers, wire transfer references, or clearing document numbers.
The base amount before tax and adjustments, used for payment terms calculation and cash discount determination.
The business area or division to which this line item is allocated. Supports segment reporting and divisional P&L analysis.
reference: IFRS 8 Operating Segments, SAP S/4HANA Business Area Accounting

description: The date on which this line item was cleared (matched with a payment or offsetting entry). Null if the item remains open.

The method used to measure progress toward complete satisfaction of the performance obligation. Cost-to-cost is the primary method for construction contracts, but other methods may be used when appropriate.
The accounting period (YYYY-MM format) for which revenue is being recognized under the percentage-of-completion method.

Cumulative retention amount withheld by the client from progress billings, typically released upon contract completion or after the Defects Liability Period (DLP). Represents a contract asset under IFRS 15.

Current status of the revenue recognition entry in the financial workflow. Draft entries are pending review; posted entries are committed to the GL; adjusted entries have been modified; reversed entries have been backed out; final entries are locked after contract completion.

Revenue recognized in the current accounting period, calculated as the difference between revenue recognized to date and revenue recognized in all prior periods. This is the amount posted to the General Ledger (GL) for the period.
Cumulative revenue recognized from contract inception through the end of the current recognition period, calculated as contract value multiplied by completion percentage.
The document number or reference identifier in the source system (e.g., SAP RAR document number) that generated this revenue recognition entry.
The operational system from which this revenue recognition entry was sourced, typically SAP S/4HANA Revenue Accounting and Reporting (RAR) module or Viewpoint Vista.

Revenue recognized but not yet billed to the client (contract asset), calculated as revenue recognized to date minus billed to date. A positive value indicates work performed ahead of billing; a negative value indicates over-billing (contract liability).
User ID or name of the person who created this revenue recognition entry record.
